claims.claim: LiveKit room output uses rtc.AudioSource(sample_rate, num_channels, queue_size_ms=200) = 200ms playout queue
Pipecat WS server computes _send_interval = (audio_chunk_size / sample_rate) / 2 to pace sends, comment 'emulate an audio device'
claims.found: self._send_interval = (self.audio_chunk_size / self.sample_rate) / 2 (line 306); comment 'we want to block to emulate an audio device, this is what the send interval is' at lines 270-273

claims.claim: Pipecat WebRTC RawAudioTrack.recv computes wait = start + timestamp/sample_rate - now and sleeps to clock frames to wall time
claims.found: wait = self._start + (self._timestamp / self._sample_rate) - time.time() (line 135); if wait > 0: await asyncio.sleep(wait)

claims.claim: LiveKit pre-connect audio buffer with max_delta_s=1.0 staleness guard, replayed once subscribed
claims.found: line 22: def __init__(self, room, *, timeout, max_delta_s: float = 1.0); line 79: if (delta := time.time() - buf.timestamp) > self._max_delta_s: ... 'pre-connect audio buffer is too old'. Frontmatter :21 = class PreConnectAudioHandler: (within range).

claims.claim: Deepgram: short jitter buffer around 100 milliseconds is usually sufficient to smooth minor network variation without adding perceptible delay
claims.found: lines 517-519: 'synthesized speech should be streamed incrementally and buffered just enough to prevent playback gaps. A short jitter buffer around 100 milliseconds is usually sufficient to smooth minor network variation without adding perceptible delay.'

claims.claim: PSTN adds an unavoidable 100-200 ms RTT floor
claims.found: lines 724-725: 'Even with optimal alignment, PSTN interactions typically add 100-200 milliseconds of round-trip delay.' (the number wraps onto line 725; 724 is within a few lines)

claims.claim: Pipecat has no separate jitter-buffer object; WS relies on send-side pacing + client buffer, WebRTC uses aiortc/Opus buffer
claims.found: WS pacing via _send_interval sleep (server.py); WebRTC recv() sleeps to wall-clock the frames (transport.py); no dedicated jitter-buffer class observed in these transport files.

claims.claim: Send-side pacing prevents flooding because TCP socket would otherwise dump all TTS at once
claims.found: comment lines 269-273: 'write_audio_frame() is called quickly ... since this is just a network connection we would be sending it to[o] quickly. Instead, we want to block to emulate an audio device'

summary: All 4 code_refs and both Deepgram source figures verified against ground truth. Every cited line shows the claimed mechanism/value. PSTN 100-200ms number wraps from line 724 to 725 (within range, not a miscitation). No WRONG or MISCITED claims.
